title: Rustic Crumbly Vietnamese Gelatin Noodles
description: A cozy and funky Vietnamese dish featuring unflavored gelatin and dashi extract, made with ayurvedic rajasic friendly ingredients. This recipe is perfect for a cold winter night.

ingredients.name: unflavored gelatin
ingredients.notes: make sure to use a high quality gelatin

instructions: In a small bowl, sprinkle the gelatin over 1/4 cup of cold water and let it sit for 5 minutes to soften.
In a large pot, combine the dashi extract, coconut milk, and 2 cups of water, and bring to a boil over high heat.
Add the softened gelatin to the pot and stir until it is fully dissolved.
Add the rice noodles to the pot and cook for 5 minutes, or until they are tender.
In a small pan, heat the vegetable oil over medium heat and add the grated ginger, cooking for 1 minute.
Add the tofu to the pan and cook until it is golden brown on all sides.
Add the cooked noodles, tofu, and ginger to a large bowl and stir to combine.
Top the noodles with scallions, sesame seeds, salt, and black pepper, and serve hot.
Let the dish cool to room temperature before refrigerating or freezing.
When reheating, make sure the dish is heated to an internal temperature of 165 degrees Fahrenheit.
